variables.short_definition: How deeply the role engages with DC system engineering details, from general awareness to hands-on design, validation, protection, commissioning, and troubleshooting.
variables.why_it_matters: Medium and low voltage DC elicitation needs roles that can distinguish between broad coordination, regulatory framing, and deep technical judgment. This dimension helps separate generalists from SMEs and mid-level from senior technical experts when assessing whether an LLM role can reason about converters, protection, controls, testbeds, and interoperability.

scoring_guidance_1_to_3.1: Broad, non-specialist understanding; can discuss DC systems at a high level but not technical design or validation details.
scoring_guidance_1_to_3.2: Working technical familiarity; can interpret and coordinate around DC system components, requirements, or project constraints.
scoring_guidance_1_to_3.3: Deep specialist expertise; can design, validate, troubleshoot, or make authoritative judgments on DC system behavior and integration.

variables.short_definition: How strongly the role focuses on regulations, standards, permitting, compliance controls, documentation discipline, and alignment with formal approval processes.
variables.why_it_matters: DC adoption depends not only on technology but also on regulatory alignment, standardization, and market readiness. This dimension captures whether a role is mainly execution-oriented or is able to shape compliance pathways, standards interpretation, and approval readiness across projects and jurisdictions.

scoring_guidance_1_to_3.1: Limited involvement in formal governance; mainly follows existing rules and procedures.
scoring_guidance_1_to_3.2: Regularly works with compliance, standards, or approval requirements and helps translate them into project actions.
scoring_guidance_1_to_3.3: Strong authority or leadership in regulatory, standards, or governance matters; shapes compliance strategy, approval pathways, or standard-setting.

variables.short_definition: How much the role operates across disciplines and stakeholder groups to align technical, commercial, operational, regulatory, and end-user perspectives.
variables.why_it_matters: DC systems are socio-technical and demonstration-heavy, so success depends on coordination across engineers, project teams, regulators, customers, operators, and communities. This dimension helps score whether an LLM role can bridge functions, manage trade-offs, and translate between technical and non-technical viewpoints.

scoring_guidance_1_to_3.1: Primarily works within a narrow function or task scope with limited stakeholder coordination.
scoring_guidance_1_to_3.2: Frequently coordinates across several functions or external parties and translates between perspectives.
scoring_guidance_1_to_3.3: Operates as a high-level integrator or interface owner, aligning multiple stakeholder groups and resolving cross-functional trade-offs.
